hawkish_phrases: inflation had moved up since earlier in the year and remained somewhat elevated
overall inflation had been above target for some time and had shown little sign of returning sustainably to the 2 percent objective in a timely manner
concerns that the prolonged period of above-target inflation could risk an increase in longer-term expectations
upside risks to inflation remained elevated
further policy rate reductions could add to the risk of higher inflation becoming entrenched
dovish_phrases: downside risks to employment had increased since earlier in the year
job gains had slowed this year and the unemployment rate had edged up
a labor market that had gradually softened through September and October but had not sharply deteriorated
downside risks to employment had risen in recent months
moving to a more neutral policy stance, the Committee was helping forestall the possibility of a major deterioration in labor market conditions
summary: The Committee voted 10-2 to cut the target range by 25 basis points to 3.75-4.00 percent, with one dissenter preferring a 50bp cut and another preferring no change, citing elevated inflation but rising downside risks to employment amid a gradually cooling labor market.
